mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly increasing present value of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenues and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which translates into higher corporate profit growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >50% of cases over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values indicate expanding manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, generating short-term positive momentum in equity markets over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when INDPRO trends upward.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above 0.4% MoM are followed by positive SPX returns in >65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict direction with an accuracy above 52% over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic activity and corporate earnings potential, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth periods show negative or flat 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals manufacturing contraction and weakening supply-side momentum, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production shocks are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in >55% of cases.
